claim: The empirical invD_power template (log-log OLS; predictions back-transformed to A for LOOCV) achieves lower LOOCV_RMSE than the best simple T baseline (T_linear) for both A_obs_canon and A_svd_full_oriented_candidate.
evidence: AX-18C scaling_law_comparison: invD_power beats_best_T_baseline=YES for both targets; judgement CSV lists invD_power as best_empirical_scaling_family.
caveat: n=15 diagnostic LOOCV only; not confirmatory.
allowed_wording: Within the AX-18C tested templates on n=15, invD_power improved LOOCV relative to T_linear (see scaling table).
forbidden_wording: The data establish a power-law scaling law in invD.
notes: Closure does not run new fits.

claim: The T_power and Xeff_power templates improve LOOCV relative to T_linear for these targets in AX-18C.
evidence: scaling_law_comparison: T_power and Xeff_power have beats_best_T_baseline=NO for both targets (relative LOOCV > 1 vs T_linear).
caveat: Does not mean T or Xeff are irrelevant in other model classes (e.g. linear coordinate models in T_function table).
allowed_wording: T_power and Xeff_power (as single log-log monomials with LOOCV in A space) did not beat T_linear in this audit.
forbidden_wording: T_power and Xeff_power are disproven as predictors in any form.

claim: invD_power is the best LOOCV scaling candidate among the families listed in scaling_law_comparison for each target.
evidence: For each target, invD_power row has the minimum LOOCV_RMSE across all rows in that target's block; matches judgement best_empirical_scaling_family=invD_power.
caveat: Only within the fixed AX-18C candidate set.
allowed_wording: Best among the tested scaling templates in AX-18C (not a search over all possible forms).
forbidden_wording: Optimal model among all possible scaling laws.

claim: A universal physical scaling law (mechanism-based) is established for A vs invD or T.
evidence: AX-18C status: empirical_fit_only; physical_scaling_law_claim=NO_NOT_FROM_THIS_AUDIT in judgement; EMPIRICAL forms only in source.
forbidden_wording: We have established a physical scaling law; the exponent is universal.

claim: All simple scaling laws are ruled out (as descriptive fits).
evidence: Several candidates are useful or competitive; invD_power beats T_linear. Ruled-out phrasing is too broad.

claim: All tested single-term log-log scaling templates are ruled out as physical laws.
evidence: No row in scaling_law_comparison is marked as physical law; all carry empirical-only flag in source column.
allowed_wording: No tested template in AX-18C supports a physical scaling-law claim.
forbidden_wording: We have disproven every possible scaling story.

claim: invD_power exponents (alpha) are numerically ~0.5625 (A_obs) and ~0.5583 (A_svd) as reported in AX-18C.
caveat: Exponents are log-log OLS on n=15; not stable without resampling study.
allowed_wording: Reported exponents are descriptive log-log slopes from AX-18C; interpret cautiously.
forbidden_wording: The exponent is a material constant.

claim: Endpoint or exponent stability (leave-one-out endpoint sensitivity) is established for invD_power.
evidence: No endpoint_stable or resampling column in AX-18C scaling artifacts; inventory marks NOT_RECORDED.
forbidden_wording: Our scaling exponent is stable to endpoint removal.
notes: Next work if stronger claims are needed.

claim: Fixed-Tc log| T - Tc | forms at 23 K, 25 K, 31.5 K are strong empirical predictors of A in this domain.
evidence: LOOCV_RMSE vastly larger than T_linear for 23/25 K; 31.5 K still loses to T_linear in table.
caveat: Domain may be far from putative criticality.
allowed_wording: The fixed-Tc single-term forms tested in AX-18C perform poorly vs T_linear here.
forbidden_wording: Critical slowing down at 23/25 K is confirmed.

claim: The empirical templates absT_minus_* and shifted_T_minus_* (except degenerate overlap with T_power at T0=0) beat T_linear.
evidence: beats_best_T_baseline=NO for all such rows in scaling table for both targets.
forbidden_wording: Shifted-T scaling explains the data better than linear T.

claim: Linear-in-coordinate models (separate T_function table) can beat T_linear even when Xeff_power log-log does not.
evidence: T_function_model_comparison: coord linear predictors have LOOCV_RMSE_relative_to_best_T_baseline < 1 for Xeff and invD vs T_linear; distinct model class from Xeff_power.
forbidden_wording: Log-log Xeff and linear Xeff must agree on rankings.
